title: 'The war in Iran is limited to three countries, but the list of collateral victims keeps growing'
subhead: Since the start of the conflict in the Middle East, the specter of powerlessness has affected many countries, and what was supposed to be a regional war has become a global crisis, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
'Kevin Warsh's nomination to the Fed reinforces the impression that an oligarchy runs the US'
French businesses will (mostly) remain closed on Labor Day as government backs down
subhead: Despite efforts by MPs from President Emmanuel Macron's party to allow local businesses such as bakeries and flower shops to open on May 1, Labor Day will remain a mandatory paid holiday in 2026.

title: Firing of respected French publisher demonstrates billionaire Bolloré's tightening grip
subhead: Olivier Nora, the long-time CEO of the Grasset publishing house, was fired on Tuesday. He was replaced with a close associate of Vincent Bolloré, who took control of France's leading publishing group Hachette Livre in 2023.

title: Calder at the Fondation Louis Vuitton in Paris, a festive experience
subhead: The exhibition spans the sculptor's entire body of work and takes over the entire building, spilling out onto the exterior. The journey is a joyful celebration, filled with lightness and movement.

title: The Curtis Yarvin conundrum: Should one debate with the far right?
subhead: The reactionary American blogger close to the Trumpian figures was invited to a symposium in Germany with prominent scholars. Some critics say the invitation signals that red lines have vanished from intellectual debate.

subhead: Being a victim is neither a profession nor their identity. Meta, Cristina, Doris, Stéphanie, Mari and Toon live in PFAS contamination hotspots in six European countries.

title: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
subhead: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

title: Investigators denied access to Elysée Palace in events contracts probe
